request.user_query: My employer has not paid my salary for 3 months. What legal action can I take in India?

example_response.answer: Under Indian labour laws, non-payment of salary is a serious violation. You have several legal remedies available...
relevant_laws: Indian Penal Code — Section 406 (Criminal Breach of Trust)
example_response.explanation: The Payment of Wages Act mandates timely payment of wages and provides penalties for delays...
example_response.why_applicable: Since your employer has withheld salary for 3 months without justification, this constitutes a violation of the Payment of Wages Act...
next_steps: Send a written demand/legal notice to your employer
File complaint with the Labour Commissioner
If unresolved, file a case in Labour Court under Section 33C of Industrial Disputes Act
Consult a labour law advocate for specific guidance

structured_data.reason: Complete documentation with payment proof and legal notice

example_response.summary: This consumer dispute appears to have strong supporting evidence. The complainant has documented the purchase, defects, communication attempts, and served legal notice — all of which strengthen the claim.
strong_points: Payment proof with transaction reference available
Legal notice served with proof of delivery
weak_points: Independent technical assessment of defects not yet obtained
next_steps: File complaint at District Consumer Forum (court fee minimal)
Obtain independent technical report for the laptop defects
Gather all communication records as exhibit

document_analysis.reason: Well-drafted complaint with complete facts and supporting documents

rule_flags: PAYMENT_PROOF: Payment/receipt documentation found — strengthens claim
MULTIPLE_PARTIES: Both parties identified — strengthens record

request.text: The agreement was signed on 10th January 2024. Payment of Rs. 50,000 due on 01/02/2024. Hearing scheduled for 20/05/2024.
